steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left side of the blank is a participial phrase (“Known as the Tunguska event, after a river that ran through the region,”). On the right side is an independent clause (“June 30 has been officially denoted International Asteroid Day.”).

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase must clearly modify the subject of the clause it begins.

C provides “the explosion” as the subject, which correctly matches what “Known as the Tunguska event” describes.

Choices A, B, and D provide subjects that don't match the phrase, creating dangling modifiers.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
